data.4518: WARNING: Non-fatal errors occured while parsing this preset. A common reason for this is that one or more forms which it references could not be resolved due to missing plugins. It's strongly recommended that you resolve the issue(s) before using it. Check the log for more information.

data.6101: UI open hotkey is not configured
data.1000: If enabled and attachment mode is set to 'Reference', the item actively moves, rotates and scales with the target node. Used for keeping in sync with managed nodes, for instance when an armor weapon offset is applied. Minimal overhead, reacts instantly.
data.1001: Drop item to the ground when the actor dies. Purely visual, same as vanilla behavior for equipped gear.
data.1002:
Determines where on the skeleton the item 3D is attached. Tags:

[W]    - Weapon node.

[D]    - Default weapon sheath node. XPMSSE moves these around the skeleton based on your style settings.

[M]    - Vanilla weapon sheath node. The game cleans up anything found on these nodes after unequippping, so we can only attach in reference mode.

[X]    - Added by XPMSSE.

[C]    - A copy of the vanilla sheath node added automatically by IED. Useful with weapons types which the game places on common nodes (two-handed swords/axes and bows/crossbows)

[E]    - Added by the user.
data.1003: Hide this item when the actor is using furniture. This includes forges, tanning racks, ore veins, etc..
data.1004:
Node attachment mode:

Parent    - Attach the 3D model directly to the target node. Not available for managed nodes.

Reference - Create a separate node on the targets' parent, attach the 3D model to it and copy position, rotation and scale from the target node. This is a workaround for placing items on vanilla sheath (managed) nodes, since the game cleans up anything that's attached after an item is unequipped. Visually there's no difference. If the target node moves to another parent, the model moves with it. Optionally tick 'Sync reference' to actively synchronize position, rotation and scale.
data.1007: Only appears if the actor carries the item in inventory.
data.1008: Load 3D model from this form and use the primary only to evaluate conditions.
data.1009:
Item shows if these min/max inventory count conditions are met. Set to 0 to disable. Useful if you want to load different models based on how large a stack is, for example larger pouches with more amount of gold.

Hint: CTRL+Click to type in the value.
data.1010: Primary item. Its 3D model will be used unless model swap form is set. Always used when evaluating conditions.
data.1011: Prefer displaying this item over the last equipped.
data.1012: Import skips temporary references. Don't turn this off unless you know what you're doing.
data.1013: Hide the object but keep the model and textures loaded.
data.1014: Propagate changes to equipment overrides. Only applies when directly editing a value.
data.1015: Disable hide-on-equip optimization for this slot. Displayed 3D model will always unload when an item is equipped. Untick 'Keep equipped items loaded' in settings to disable globally.
data.1016:
Optimization: when an item is equipped, hide its displayed equipment slot 3D model instead of unloading it. Eliminates any potential hitches resulting from loading assets when switching between items. The tradeoff is higher memory usage.

Note that there's no benefit if the switched-to item is different than the one currently loaded in its designated slot.
data.1017: Load left scabbard if the selected model is a weapon.
data.1018: Disable hide-on-equip optimization for this item. Displayed 3D model will always unload when the item is equipped or slotted in an equipment display.
data.1019: Hide this item when the actor is laying down (beds and lay-down markers).
data.1020: If the loaded model is a weapon, remove its scabbard.
data.1021: Force loading first-person weapon meshes. This is always true for player.
data.1022: The item won't be displayed if it's flagged as 'cannot wear' (prevent equip).
data.1023: The item will have a random chance of loading when first evaluated or if a reset is triggered.
data.1024: The item will behave similarly to an equipment display; race equipment flags are honored (optional) and equipped state is accounted for. Unless 'Disable if equipped' is ticked, the item may still show when equipped if an excess amount is carried in the inventory (arrows are an exception).
data.1025: Equipment mode ignores race equip types. Note that the base option for this is honored as well.
data.1026: Never show this display if the item is equipped.
data.1027:
Accelerates loads by caching recently used models. Higher values keep a higher amount of unused models in cache which uses up more memory (particularly VRAM since textures remain loaded).

'Used only' evicts unused but keeps original instances of loaded models in cache so attaching duplicates is much faster. Since these only need to be cloned, no disk I/O can happen. Texture swaps may still need to be loaded if present.

'Disabled' turns off caching completely.
data.1028: Close the UI if escape key is pressed.
data.1029: Block the UI popup when in combat, pursued by guards, in a conversation, etc..
data.1030: Prevent all user input (mouse, keyboard, gamepad) from reaching the game when the UI is active.
data.1031: Scales font size as well as other UI elements (mouse cursor, padding, scrollbars, ...). First set an appropriate size for your font (ensure it fits the elements well) then use this to scale everything.
data.1032: If you use XPMSSE styles, set up placements exactly the same in both mods. This plugin cannot switch animations on its own, if you want that functionality you still need the XPMSEE plugin.
data.1034:
Overwrite: Replace current configuration with imported data

Merge: Copy individual actor/npc/race entries into current configuration (with overwriting). Global entries are overwritten.
data.1035: If the item is an armor, load valid addons instead of the world model.
data.1036: Automatically select the actor/npc/race in player crosshairs where applicable.
data.1037: By default torch flame geometry and effects are removed when loading meshes from TESObjectLIGH records, this keeps them attached. Vanila torch lights are never attached, but it's possible for ENB light to show up on custom torches.
data.1038: EXPERIMENTAL: Disable model collision. May be necessary for certain meshes if they collide with the camera or world objects.
data.1039: Match form in selected biped slot against the actors' current skin armor. Form negator still applies.
data.1040:
Synchronize config between sexes. Only applies when directly editing a value, it has no effect when importing since both male and female configs are always stored in presets.

Ignore preset installation instructions which tell you to tick this. You'll normally want this OFF, especially when editing a record which has unique settings for each sex otherwise the opposite sex config will get clobbered.
data.1041: The item won't show unless favorited.
data.1042: Equipment shows up on the player even if not favorited.
data.1043: Freeze time while the UI is open.
data.1044: Use the world model when loading shields. Necessary when attaching shields to actors whose race isn't normally listed in ARMA records, horses for example.
data.1045: Fonts with large glyph sets (Chinese, Japanese, ..) can produce large atlas textures which use up a lot of VRAM (even in excess of 100MB), this frees up that memory by releasing the texture after the UI closes. Not necessary with the default font or if no extra glyphs are mapped since the overhead is marginal.
data.1046: Ignore race equip types. Primarily meant for equipment slots.
data.1047: Evaluates to true if form/keyword matches the current location or one of its parents.
data.1048: If both form and keyword are set, evaluates to true if either match. If unchecked, both must match.
data.1049: If the actor is in a small worldspace, match against its parent as well.
data.1050: Plays the specified animation sequence on the loaded model. Not to be confused with XPMSSE draw/sheathe animations.
data.1051:
Equipped: run condition on equipped item(s)

Displayed: run condition on displayed equipment slot(s). Does not apply to custom items. In equipment view it only matches against the item in the slot where it's configured, however in custom and gear positioning views this matches against all slots.

Keep 'Or' ticked to match either.

In equipment and custom views you can untick both to test if the item is present in the inventory. However, gear positioning conditions do not scan inventory contents (biped slots are used instead) so unticking both options will always evaluate to false.
